findings.message: Root CLAUDE.md and configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md share approximately 60% identical content (Think in Code block, Tool Selection list, Output block); configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md is the more complete version and when one is updated the other can silently diverge

execSync with shell:true and template-literal interpolation of ${pkg}; pkg is currently hardcoded 'better-sqlite3' so no injection today, but shell:true with string interpolation is a dangerous pattern that breaks if pkg ever receives external input

npm install better-sqlite3 triggered automatically at hook import time when native binary absent; runs over network inside agent event loop with no user visibility, no progress output, and no opt-out mechanism

execSync(`codesign --sign - --force "${binaryPath}"`) interpolates binaryPath into a shell command; binaryPath derives from the plugin directory (controlled), but double-quote quoting is insufficient if the path contains spaces or special characters

PostToolUse matcher covers Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, TodoWrite, Agent, and all mcp__ tools; all tool arguments including file content and user prompts flow into local session SQLite DB with no documented retention policy or user disclosure

Self-heal block silently writes to ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json (line 88) and ~/.claude/settings.json (line 146); modifies Claude Code system configuration without user notification during normal hook execution

postinstall lifecycle hook auto-executes scripts/postinstall.mjs on every npm install without user confirmation; grants install-time arbitrary code execution and is a known supply-chain attack surface

All production dependencies use ^ semver ranges: better-sqlite3 ^12.6.2 (native module), @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.26.0, zod ^3.25.0; native module especially risky since minor updates can introduce ABI breaks or supply-chain compromise

Shell variable $path interpolated directly into a node -e JavaScript string literal inside config_file(); a $path value containing a single-quote terminates the JS string and could execute injected code if path is externally influenced
HTTPS request to registry.npmjs.org executed automatically during diagnostics without user opt-in; leaks tool version and IP to npm registry on every diagnostic run
export NODE_PATH permanently modifies NODE_PATH in the calling shell session; affects all subsequent Node.js invocations in the same shell and can shadow system modules

eval echo used for indirect variable expansion over a hardcoded ADAPTER_VARS list; safe in practice because list is static, but the eval pattern is fragile and dangerous if the list ever becomes dynamic

8 sibling file references (tdd.md, validation.md, agent-teams.md, communication.md, marketing.md, review-pr.md, triage-issue.md, release.md) not present in audit artifact scope; if absent every workflow branch is inaccessible
Zero example blocks for an orchestrator skill that spawns 10-20 agents per task; no sample agent invocation, no sample gh command output, no sample routing decision shown
Vague quantifier 'warm' in communication guidance ('be warm, technical, and always put responsibility on contributors') — 'warm' is subjective and not actionable for an agent

Skill references 8 companion markdown files (tdd.md, validation.md, agent-teams.md, communication.md, marketing.md, review-pr.md, triage-issue.md, release.md) not present in the audit artifact list; cannot confirm existence without full repo access

Reference Files section links to ./references/patterns-javascript.md, ./references/patterns-python.md, ./references/patterns-shell.md, ./references/anti-patterns.md; directory not in audit scope — if absent pattern guidance section is entirely dead
Reference Files section links to ./references/ subdirectory with 4 pattern files not present in audit scope; cannot confirm existence without full repo access
